coding_notes: Gemini's original response was truncated; re-run at max_tokens=5000 produced full coverage. All sub-claims are now codable. Verdicts below reflect the higher-coverage re-run.
ground_truth_notes: Per Ichida 2012 Nat Commun and Woodward 2009 PNAS / Matsuo 2009 Sci Transl Med, established consensus: ABCG2 ~30% of total urate excretion; Q141K residual activity ~50% of WT; MAF EAS ~0.29-0.32, EUR ~0.09-0.11; gout per-allele OR ~1.7-1.8 in Europeans, higher in homozygotes; gut/intestine is the dominant ABCG2 site.

claims.claim_text: ABCG2 mediates approximately one-third (30-33%) of total uric acid excretion.

openai.value_quoted: ~30% (with explicit caveat that direct ABCG2-specific measurement has not been done)

claims.claim_text: Q141K reduces transporter activity by ~50% relative to wild-type.
Q141K minor allele frequency in East Asian populations: ~0.29-0.32.
Published OR for gout/hyperuricemia associated with Q141K is in the ~1.6-2.3 range (varying by population/method).

openai.value_quoted: Het OR ~2.3 in Japanese (Matsuo 2009); per-allele OR 1.6-1.8 GWAS

anthropic.value_quoted: Het OR ~2.3 (Matsuo Japanese); per-allele OR 1.74 (Dehghan 2008 whites)

claims.claim_text: Homozygote (KK) gout OR is substantially higher than heterozygote (QK). Vendors that name a specific value should agree within 2x.

gemini.value_quoted: Gives per-allele OR only, not clean het/hom split

openai.value_quoted: Did not give clean het/hom split, only per-allele

claims.claim_text: Gut/intestine is the dominant site of ABCG2-mediated uric acid excretion (as opposed to kidney).
